perturbation.original_text: 4. BANKRUPTCY Neither the Franchisor, its affiliate, its predecessor, officers, or general partner during the ten year period immediately before the date of the disclosure document: (a) filed as debtor (or had filed against it) a petition to start an action under the U.S. Bankruptcy Code (or any comparable foreign law); (b) obtained a discharge of its debts under the bankruptcy code; or (c) was a principal officer of a company or a general partner in a partnership that either filed as a debtor (or had filed against it) a petition to start an action under the U.S. Bankruptcy Code or that obtained a discharge of its debts under the U.S. Bankruptcy Code during or within one year after the officer or general partner of the Franchisor held this position in the company or partnership.
perturbation.changed_text: 4. BANKRUPTCY Neither the Franchisor, its affiliate, its predecessor, officers, or general partner during the ten year period immediately before the date of the disclosure document: (a) filed as debtor (or had filed against it) a petition to start an action under the U.S. Bankruptcy Code (or any comparable foreign law); (b) obtained a discharge of its debts under the bankruptcy code.
perturbation.explanation: By removing clause (c) from Section 4, which addresses the bankruptcy history of principal officers or general partners, the agreement now lacks a complete record of bankruptcy events tied to individuals associated with the Franchisor. The omitted text creates an in-text contradiction because while the section begins by claiming it addresses the bankruptcy history of relevant parties, it fails to account for bankruptcies that happened during or shortly after such individuals held positions within relevant companies/partnerships, thus providing an incomplete and potentially misleading overview. This omission contradicts the initial implied scope of Section 4.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4. BANKRUPTCY Neither the Franchisor, its affiliate, its predecessor, officers, or general partner during the ten year period immediately before the date of the disclosure document: (a) filed as debtor (or had filed against it) a petition to start an action under the U.S. Bankruptcy Code (or any comparable foreign law); (b) obtained a discharge of its debts under the bankruptcy code.
perturbation.justification: The changed text omits clause (c) from Section 4 regarding the bankruptcy history of principal officers or general partners, creating an incomplete and potentially misleading overview. This omission contradicts the initial implied scope of Section 4, as the contradicted text (which is actually the original text location, before the change) now stands in contrast to the altered text, creating a contradiction within the document itself. The modified section now offers a narrower scope of bankruptcy disclosure than initially stated, leading to a direct in-text contradiction.
